title: crataegus brazoria var. viburnifolia
intro: Crataegus brazoria var. viburnifolia, also known as the Texas hawthorn or viburnifolia hawthorn, is a variant of the Crataegus brazoria species, a type of hawthorn shrub native to the southwestern United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that may help protect against cardiovascular disease and other health conditions. The health benefits of Crataegus brazoria var. viburnifolia are thought to include lowering blood pressure, improving circulation, and reducing the risk of heart disease, among other potential benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, flowers, and berries are typically used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
